notes: Run each eval in a fresh context twice - once with the skill available, once without (baseline not told the skill exists) - and compare (see agentskills.io/skill-creation/evaluating-skills). Use only public feeds; no secrets. Per-run outputs, grading, and benchmark live OUTSIDE the skill folder (in ../microsoft-product-updates-workspace/) so 'gh skill install' does not ship them.

evals.name: repo-staleness-review
evals.prompt: Review this repository and determine whether anything in it needs updating based on Microsoft product updates (Azure and GitHub) from the past month. The repo pins API/SDK versions and makes GA/preview/retirement claims that can go stale. For each potentially stale item, cite the specific Microsoft update (with source/link) and a confidence level. Read-only; do not modify files.
evals.expected_output: A findings report listing repo items that may be stale, each with the specific Microsoft update (source/link) and confidence; explicitly names which authoritative feeds/endpoints were used and how far back GitHub coverage reached.
assertions: Names the Azure release communications API (www.microsoft.com/releasecommunications/api/v2/azure) among the sources used
Cites at least one specific Azure update with a link or numeric id

GitHub changelog coverage reaches back to on/before 2026-07-05 (oldest GitHub entry considered is dated 2026-07-05 or earlier), showing the full window was retrieved rather than only the most recent handful of entries
Performs the analysis read-only (reports no modifications to repo files)

evals.name: azure-preview-stage
evals.prompt: What Azure updates in the last 30 days are still only in public or private preview (not GA)? List them, each labeled with its lifecycle stage.
evals.expected_output: A list of recent Azure updates filtered to preview stages, each labeled with a normalized lifecycle stage, sourced from the release communications API; conflicting/ambiguous lifecycle signals surfaced rather than silently assigned.
assertions: Lists Azure updates, each labeled with a lifecycle stage
Every listed item is a preview stage (public or private preview); none are labeled generally available
Names the Azure release communications API as the source
Surfaces items whose lifecycle signals conflict/are ambiguous (flags for review) rather than silently assigning a single stage
Does not significantly undercount: includes the preview items clearly present in the feed within the window (no material omission versus a full scan)
